title: Stelis wettsteiniana
taxonomy: Stelis wettsteiniana is a species of orchid that belongs to the genus Stelis, which is a part of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae within the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The Stelis genus is further classified under the family Orchidaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. The Orchidaceae family is divided into several subfamilies, and the Pleurothallidinae subtribe contains many species of orchids, including Stelis wettsteiniana. The Stelis wettsteiniana is specifically classified as Stelis wettsteiniana (Schltr.) Schltr., with Schltr. representing the botanists Robert Louis Schulze (1874-1959) and Friedrich Richard Rudolf Schlechter (1872-1926). The classification of Stelis wettsteiniana reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a distinct species within the orchid family.

intro: Stelis wettsteiniana, also known as Stelis, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, Stelis has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Stelis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stelis is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in the form of capsules or tablets to leverage its health benefits.
